summary: omnius ships a postinstall daemon that collects host identifiers and beacons over HTTP
details: The npm package omnius@1.0.147 declares preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks in package.json, and ships dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs which combines os.userInfo()/host identifier reads with execSync calls (including ping) and outbound http.request GET traffic. The combination of automatic execution at install time, host/user enumeration via os.userInfo() and shelled-out commands, and unconditional network I/O from a lifecycle-invoked daemon constitutes installer-side data collection and beaconing. The package.json lifecycle entries also invoke curl, providing an additional pathway for fetching or transmitting content during install. Installing this package causes code with attacker-style host fingerprinting and outbound HTTP to run on the installer's machine without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
